column5: Notes

column2: Continuous page scroll (all pages stacked browser-style)
column5: Re-rated Mediumâ†’Hard (2026-06): the single-page assumption is load-bearing everywhere â€” `_screen_to_pdf` would become screenâ†’(page,x,y), every gesture/zoom/search/anchor path assumes one current page, and 'current page' drives the notes panel (scroll would constantly commit/restore notes while typing). Several sessions, high regression risk. Prerequisite: pageâ†’surface LRU render cache (shared with #23 thumbnails). Cheap alternative shipped as #20.

column2: Text selection (Alt+drag copies text like a browser)
column5: `Alt+drag` highlights words (word-level via `page.get_text`('words')); `Ctrl+C` copies in reading order with line/paragraph breaks. `Alt+click` opens PDF links. Status was stale â€” shipped in May.
Needs ocrmypdf installed; requires progress indicator for large files. Unchanged.
Live Typora-style rendering implemented with GtkSource TextTags; `Ctrl+B`/I/E formatting shortcuts added.
Errors show in `Adw.AlertDialog` with a Copy Error button (fixed GTK4 clipboard API).

column2: MX Master thumb button (btn 277) toggles pan mode
column5: Hold-to-pan (2026-06): hold button 10 and move the mouse to pan, release to stop (was a press-toggle via GestureSingle). Detected with EventControllerLegacy â€” the only layer that reports button-10 press AND release through pointer movement, since gestures cancel the sequence once a drag claims it (`extras/probe_thumb.py`). PyGObject quirk: the signal's event arg marshals as None, so the handler falls back to `controller.get_current_event()`.
Added alongside thumb button work.

column2: Set as default application for PDFs
column5: .desktop file registered via xdg-mime; also registered for text/markdown.

column2: Auto-convert PPTX to PDF on open
column5: LibreOffice runs in background thread; shows Converting toast.
Migrated from Poppler to PyMuPDF; strokes saved as PDF ink annotations â€” individually erasable across sessions.
`Ctrl+Shift+N` inserts blank page; `Ctrl+Shift+Del` removes current page.
Intercepts close-request and open-file when dirty; Save / Discard / Cancel.
Requires matplotlib mathtext â†’ Cairo pixmap â†’ TextBuffer child anchor; manageable but needs TextMark bookkeeping for cursor-show/hide like Typora.

Shipped (2026-06): drag a thumbnail onto another in the page-thumbnail sidebar (#32) to reorder. `PDFCanvas.move_page` reorders the doc via `doc.select`(order) and re-keys all_strokes/_anchors and the undo/redo stacks; `NotesModel.reorder` and `_move_page` re-key notes + the undo timelines. Thumbnail rows carry a `Gtk.DragSource`/`Gtk.DropTarget` (int payload) â€” intra-app DnD, no portal. Export part already covered by save/#18.

column2: Anchor markers linking notes to PDF spots
column5: `Ctrl+Alt+click` inserts `<!-- anchor:X:Y -->` at notes cursor; numbered circles drawn on canvas. Stored invisibly in the sidecar .md file. Cursor line in notes highlights the corresponding marker on the PDF; clicking a marker jumps the notes cursor to its line.

SearchEntry in a slide-down Revealer above the canvas. Searches all pages via `fitz.search_for`; highlights all hits in yellow, active match in orange. Next/prev with Enter/â†‘/â†“ buttons; wraps around; navigates across pages automatically.

Superscripts (x^2 or x^{ab}), subscripts (`x_1` or x_{ab}), and symbol shortcuts (\sum â†’ ÎŁ, \alpha â†’ Î±, 30+ symbols). Markers hidden on non-cursor lines; symbols substituted in buffer and restored when cursor enters. rise+scale TextTags, no matplotlib.

`Ctrl+E` exports the open PDF with a notes page after each source page. Anchor markers drawn on source pages (numbered circles); notes text rendered inline with [N] markers replacing anchors. Option to include/skip empty-notes pages.
Operation-based undo stack: `Ctrl+Z` undoes draws and erase drags (one drag = one undo group); also restores strokes loaded from file. Stack re-keyed on page insert/delete.

column2: Scroll past page boundary flips page
column5: Scrolling 3 notches past a visible page edge flips the page (resistance resets on direction reversal). Fitted views re-fit the new page; zoomed views keep zoom/horizontal position and align the new page top (bottom when flipping back). Canvas-initiated page changes now fire `on_page_will_change` so the window commits notes first â€” also fixes note loss on link jumps and cross-page undo.

Snapshot dirty PDF+notes to ~/.local/state/sidemark/autosave/<path-hash>/ every 60s via `canvas.save_copy()`; original never touched until explicit save. On open: Recover / Discard / Not now dialog when a snapshot is newer than the file. Snapshot deleted on save and on discard-changes; pruned after 30 days at startup. Limitation: notes-only mode (.md without PDF) is not autosaved yet.

column2: Callouts: anchor note text rendered on the PDF with an arrow
column5: `<!-- callout:X:Y -->` marker paired with an anchor (working name was 'ship'). `Ctrl+Alt+drag` places anchor at press and callout box at release (dashed preview line during drag); plain `Ctrl+Alt+click` stays anchor-only. Paragraph text rendered in a wrapped box on canvas (PangoCairo, screen-space for crisp text) and in `Ctrl+E` export (height measured on a scratch page â€” `insert_textbox` silently renders nothing on overflow). Shared `_parse_anchors`() feeds canvas, window highlight logic and export. Drag-to-reposition still open.

`doc.get_toc()` â†’ ListBox in a slide-out Revealer left of the split pane; rows indented by level, click navigates, `Ctrl+T` or header button toggles. Button disabled for PDFs without a TOC. Pairs with future page-thumbnail sidebar (#14).

Shipped: `recent.json` in ~/.local/share/sidemark (newest first, cap 15, scratchpad/blanks excluded); header MenuButton rebuilt on popover show; `Gtk.RecentManager` feeds `recently-used.xbel` (GTK dialogs, KF6 KRecentDocument/krunner). Walker/Omarchy: `extras/sidemark_recent.lua` elephant menu (jq-based, modeled on the local `vscode.lua`), installed via opt-in ./`install.sh` --walker-menu (xbel + default-app handling already covers most workflows); sidemark --list-recent prints name\tpath without importing GTK for other launchers. Skipped: dedicated krunner C++/KPackage plugin (xbel already covers KDE; a native runner would be ~1-2 days incl. packaging). Startup view instead of scratchpad still open.

Shipped: stroke dict gained 'opacity'; Pen/Highlighter segmented switcher at the top of the pen-settings popover (`Ctrl+H` flips it); while the highlighter is active the header pen button shows a live preview of the highlight stroke instead of the pencil icon. Pen-settings popover (width scale, color button, swatches) routes to whichever tool is active; highlighter defaults to yellow/12pt/0.40. Round-trips through the PDF CA key via `annot.set_opacity` / `annot.opacity` (fitz reports -1 when unset). Eraser, undo/redo and export work unchanged since strokes stay ordinary ink annotations.
`Gtk.GestureStylus` gives pressure, but PDF ink annotations have one width per annotation â€” variable-width strokes need segmenting into multiple annots. Needs hardware to verify.
Manifest + PyMuPDF module build is the main effort; Flathub would reach non-Arch users. No code changes.

column2: Keep PDF view adjusted when notes sidebar toggles or window resizes
column5: Canvas reacts to its GTK resize signal: while in fitted state (tracked via `_is_fitted`, cleared by any manual zoom/pan) a resize re-fits the page; a manually zoomed view keeps the PDF point at the viewport center anchored. Zoom level is never changed for zoomed views.

Single `Ctrl+Z` timeline across canvas and notes (fixes drawâ†’typeâ†’draw undoing in the wrong order when focus stayed in the sidebar). Window keeps `_undo_timeline`: one entry per draw/erase gesture (`canvas.on_user_action`) and one per typing burst between two canvas actions (token stores the page and the text before the burst). Capture-phase key controller intercepts `Ctrl+Z` before the TextView (skipped when a `Gtk.Editable` like the search entry has focus). Notes undo restores the snapshot (irreversible `set_text` â€” buffer-native undo replaced by the timeline) and jumps to the token's page. Timeline re-keyed on page insert/delete, cleared on file open. Redo added as #31.

The notes panel sits in a `Gtk.Paned` (resizable), so the outline's `Gtk.Revealer` slide can't be reused â€” instead `Adw.TimedAnimation` drives paned `set_position` over 250ms (ease-out-cubic). Box hidden only when the collapse finishes; a 600ms skip-guard finishes the slide when no frames tick (offscreen tests, hidden window).

Mirrors the global undo timeline: canvas keeps a `_redo_stack` of op-lists exactly as `undo_last` popped them (erase groups round-trip); window keeps `_redo_timeline` with notes tokens storing before+after text. Any new user action (gesture or typing burst) clears redo. Same capture-phase key controller, header button next to undo. Both stacks re-keyed on page insert/delete, cleared on file open.

column2: Page-thumbnail sidebar when the PDF has no outline
column5: Shipped (2026-06): when `get_toc`() is empty, `Ctrl+T` fills the existing `_toc_list` with 96px `Gdk.MemoryTexture` thumbnails (numbered, SINGLE selection) rendered lazily via a `GLib.idle_add` queue invalidated on document change (~0.5ms/page text, 5-30ms scans, ~110KB each). Row click jumps; page changes select + center-scroll the current thumbnail; page insert/delete repopulates. Toggle now bounces only with no document open. Follow-up shipped: Outline â‡„ Pages switcher atop the sidebar when a TOC exists, and the sidebar narrows to hug the thumbnails in Pages view (230px for outline text). Strokes drawn after opening are not yet reflected in the thumbnails â€” live refresh plus page-reorder drag (#14) can build on a shared render cache later (also the prerequisite cache for #1 continuous scroll).

column2: Thumb button + scroll wheel zooms instead of panning
column5: Shipped (2026-06): `_on_scroll` routes to the cursor-anchored zoom path while `_thumb_panning` is latched; the thumb pan origin is rebased after each zoom step so the next motion event doesn't jump. Now hold-based together with #6 â€” hold the thumb button and scroll to zoom, release to stop (was a press-toggle), via the EventControllerLegacy press/release detection documented in #6.
Shipped (2026-06): btn==2 branch in `_on_drag_begin` sets the same _panning state as the `Ctrl+drag` path; drag-update/end pan handling needed no changes.

Shipped (2026-06): PageUp/PageDown and mouse buttons 8/9 route through `window._nav_page` â†’ `canvas._flip_page`, the same path as #20 scroll-past-edge flips: fitted views re-fit, zoomed views keep zoom and align the new page's top (bottom when going back). Bounds-clamped no-op at first/last page.

column2: Install opt-in: register as default handler for PowerPoint files
column5: Shipped (2026-06): ./`install.sh` --register-pptx runs xdg-mime default for both PowerPoint mime types (the .desktop file already declared them). Off by default â€” most users want an office suite as their .pptx handler.

column2: Hold after drawing snaps stroke to straight line (GoodNotes-style)
column5: On motion-stop (`GLib.timeout_add` ~500 ms) activate straight-line mode: replace the in-progress polyline preview with a single line from start-point to cursor, track cursor until release, commit as a normal ink annotation. Needs a drawing state machine (free â†’ holding â†’ straight) and a canvas preview redraw during the hold phase without committing. No annotation format changes.

`Gtk.GestureZoom` gives a scale factor delta and handles both touchscreen pinch and touchpad pinch (GDK touchpad gesture events) in GTK4. On begin: record viewport anchor at pinch centroid (`get_bounding_box_center`); on scale-changed: apply zoom centred on that anchor â€” same math as the existing `Ctrl+scroll` zoom. Main risk: interplay with the EventControllerScroll (two-finger touchpad scroll = pan) on the same canvas; needs claim/deny tuning. Cannot test touchscreen without hardware; touchpad pinch testable locally.

column2: Drag-and-drop a file onto the window to open it
column5: Shipped (2026-06): `Gtk.DropTarget` for `Gdk.FileList` on the window; `_on_file_drop` opens the first dropped .pdf/.pptx/.md via the existing `open_file`() path (handles the unsaved-changes prompt and pptx/md dispatch). Unsupported drops show a toast.

column2: Header bar should stay visible in fullscreen
column5: Shipped (2026-06): root cause was the header sitting in the `set_titlebar`() slot, which GTK4 hides in fullscreen by design (not a Hyprland issue). Fix: window base class moved `Gtk.ApplicationWindow`â†’`Adw.ApplicationWindow`, header now added via `Adw.ToolbarView.add_top_bar`() with the toast overlay as `set_content`() â€” top bars stay visible in fullscreen. `set_child`â†’`set_content`. Regression test fullscreens the window and asserts the header stays mapped and lives under the `Adw.ToolbarView`.

column2: Toggle between draw mode and text-select mode
column5: Shipped (2026-06): canvas gained a `select_mode` flag; the modifier-less branch of `_on_drag_begin` routes to the existing `Alt+drag` text-selection state when it's set, otherwise draws. Header `Gtk.ToggleButton` + `Ctrl+M` flip it; a text cursor on the canvas indicates select mode. `Alt+drag` still selects in either mode; right-drag erase unchanged.

column2: Markdown notes: duplicate line (Ctrl+D) and move line up/down (Alt+â†‘/â†“)
column5: Shipped (2026-06): `MarkdownNotesView._on_key` gained `Ctrl+D` (duplicate the current line, or every line the selection spans) and `Alt+â†‘/â†“` (move the current or selected lines), alongside the `Ctrl+B`/I/E wrappers. The line ops rebuild the affected span by splitting on newlines â€” handling a final line with no trailing newline â€” and go through the normal changed/dirty path so the global undo timeline (#29) records them as ordinary notes edits.

column2: Ctrl+F also searches the Markdown notes panel
column5: Shipped (2026-06): unified PDF+notes search (user picked Word-style ordering â€” start on the current page, cycle forward, wrap). `_on_search_changed` commits the open note, collects `fitz.search_for` PDF hits plus `_find_note_matches` (case-insensitive substring on the stored `notes_model` text), and builds one flat list ordered by page (`("pdf",page,rect_idx)` / `("note",page,start,end)`). `_go_to_match` switches page for either kind; for note hits `_select_note_match` runs `_restore_note` so the buffer holds the raw stored text (before rehighlight substitutes \\alphaâ†’Î± on non-cursor lines) and the model offsets map exactly, then selects + scrolls. Searches the model not the buffer to dodge the symbol-substitution offset skew.

column2: /date slash command inserts today's date in notes
column5: Shipped (2026-06): `MarkdownNotesView._on_key` calls `_expand_snippet` on Space/Enter; it walks back to the whitespace-delimited token before the cursor and, if it is `/date`/`/time`/`/now`, replaces it with `datetime` output (ISO date, `%H:%M`, or both) via the normal dirty/undo path. Glued tokens like `foo/date` are left alone. Returns False so the triggering Space/Enter still inserts.

column2: Responsive header bar â€” collapse non-essential controls when narrow
column5: The top bar has grown crowded (open, recent, search, outline, pen, highlighter, select-mode, undo/redo, notes toggle, save, menu). Move secondary actions into an overflow menu and/or use `Adw.Breakpoint` (`Adw.ApplicationWindow` is already in place since #40) to collapse controls below a width threshold while keeping the core set (open, save, search, notes toggle) always visible. Could also group pen/highlighter/select into one tools popover.

column2: Drag a placed anchor to reposition it
column5: Shipped (2026-06): completes the "drag-to-reposition still open" note from #22. Pressing an anchor circle starts a drag (`_anchor_dragging`); `_on_drag_update` moves it live by mutating the parsed `_anchors` dict, and `_on_drag_end` rewrites the idx-th `<!-- anchor:X:Y -->` marker in the notes via `_on_anchor_moved` (the buffer "changed" handler re-parses to refresh the canvas). A press under the move threshold (4px) still jumps the notes cursor like before; a grab/grabbing cursor signals the affordance.
